t_ch: 约定之期已到，该告诉你们…我的真实身份了。
t_en: The appointed day has come, and it's time to tell you... my true identity.

t_ch: 其实，我是一瓶苏乐达。
t_en: I am actually a bottle of SoulGlad.

t_ch: 原来绿选手的真身是苏乐达汽水？！怪不得他始终如此自信，成竹在胸，傲视所有酒瓶！
t_en: Greenie is actually a SoulGlad bottle!? No wonder he's been exuding sheer confidence, calm and composed, lording over all other bottles!

t_ch: 只见变身后的绿选手随便摇了摇瓶身，喷涌出的无数气泡便将他推到了舞台顶端，那是何等迷人的姿态，任何酒瓶都无法与这份闪耀相媲美！
t_en: The now-transformed Greenie nonchalantly shakes its body, spraying forth countless bubbles that push him towards the top of the stage. That mesmerizing stance... no other bottle can achieve such a pinnacle of sparkling beauty!

t_ch: 喝下这瓶苏乐达，快乐美梦速速达！
t_en: Grab a bottle of SoulGlad, make your dreams a blast!

t_ch: ——全剧终。
t_en: ...The end.

t_ch: 拍摄结束了，感觉还不错吧？哎呀，没想到这位特邀演员的表现能这么出色。
t_en: The shoot's over. Not bad, eh? Ah, I never thought this specially invited actor would do so brilliantly.

t_ch: 哼，记得把无关镜头都剪掉，只留苏乐达的内容。我们按时长付费。
t_en: Hmph, don't forget to cut out the other pointless scenes, and just leave the ones with SoulGlad in. We charge by the hour.

t_ch: 你总算明白了，这并非是普通的学生电影，而是苏乐达公司的广告片，其余酒瓶不过只是个陪衬，更重要的是——你似乎也成了这场商业活动中的一环。
t_en: You finally understand — this isn't some ordinary student film. It's an ad for the SoulGlad company. The other bottles were mere ornaments. More importantly, you seem to have become a cog in the business machine.

t_ch: 好吧，名人是非多，你也该学着去习惯这种生活了。
t_en: All right. Fame comes with its fair share of drama. It's about time you get used to this life.

t_ch: 这下万策尽了……
t_en: This is really the end of the rope...

t_ch: 请先完成旁边的拼图…
t_en: Please finish the jigsaw puzzle at the side first...

t_ch: 把电影场景布置好。
t_en: Set up the film scene.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 这是你所拍摄的电影的剧照——红色酒瓶倒立在舞台的正中央，那光彩照人的模样让你莫名感到有些心酸。
t_en: This is a stage photo of the film you shot — the red bottle doing a handstand at the center of the stage, its radiance leaving you inexplicably saddened.

t_ch: 这是你所拍摄的电影的剧照——金色酒瓶如愿站在了舞台的最中央，但这真的值得吗？
t_en: This is a stage photo of the film you shot — the gold bottle standing center-stage, exactly what it wished for. But was it really worth it?

t_ch: 这是你所拍摄的电影的剧照——苏乐达汽水瓶独占了整个舞台，在它的光芒之下，任何酒瓶都黯然失色。
t_en: This is a stage photo of the film you shot — the SoulGlad soda bottle hogging the entire stage. All other bottles dimmed and faded beneath its blinding radiance.

t_ch: 眼前的装置像是熄灭的火炬，你望着它，没由来地生出一种亲切感。
t_en: The device before you resembles an extinguished torch. You look upon it and find yourself filled with an ineffable sense of affection.

t_ch: 点燃它。
t_en: Ignite it.

t_ch: 还是别乱碰了。
t_en: Don't touch it.

t_ch: 随着你手掌的贴近，一颗澄亮璀璨的球体逐渐增大，自装置底部缓缓升起。这究竟是什么原理？你对此百思不得其解。
t_en: As your hand draws close, a pure, bright spheroid expands, rising from the bottom of the device. What principles are at work here? You find yourself utterly befuddled.

t_ch: 璀璨的光球复位成功，表面金黄色的液体缓缓流动下，形成奇妙的光晕。
t_en: The spheroid has successfully returned to its original position. As the golden fluid on its surface flows slowly, it forms a wondrous halo.

t_ch: 正当你认真观察顶部「火炬」重燃的变化之时，脚下忽然传来强烈的震动——
t_en: Just as you carefully observe the changes as the "torch" at the top is reigniting, you feel strong vibrations coming from beneath your feet...

t_ch: {F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}，方才工坊传来一阵响动，我还以为是兽群生变，特意前来查看…没想到竟是你重新点燃了吉奥里亚的圣烛。
t_en: {F#Girl}{M#Boy}, I thought the commotion I felt in the workshop just now was something happening with the beasts down there, so I came rushing over... and I didn't realize you actually reignited Georios' holy candle.

t_ch: 什么情况？
t_en: What's the issue?

t_ch: 吉奥里亚的圣烛？
t_en: Georios' holy candle?

t_ch: 在老头激动的讲解下，你这才明白，眼前的圣烛是吉奥里亚的信徒所供，却又因不明原因被废弃。
t_en: Through the old man's excited explanations, you realize that this holy candle is an offering from Georios' followers, but was eventually abandoned for unknown reasons.

t_ch: 但旁边这个宝箱……
t_en: But the chest next to it...

t_ch: 我可以摸摸你的宝箱吗？
t_en: Can I touch your treasure chest?

t_ch: 这个宝箱是怎么回事？
t_en: What's up with this chest?

t_ch: 既然是你重燃圣烛，这就是吉奥里亚对你的奖赏…唔，从表面的尘土来看，这多半是深埋在地下的珍宝……
t_en: Since you rekindled the holy candle, then this must be Georios' reward... From the dust on its surface, this must be a treasure that has been buried deep...

t_ch: 吉奥里亚以其伟力勒令大地献上祝福——此类事项过往也有记载。
t_en: Georios once used divine powers to command the land to offer its bounty forcibly — Such events have also been recorded.

t_ch: 没想到传闻中的事情被我亲眼目睹…真是三生有幸。
t_en: I just didn't expect to witness the legends with my own eyes... I am truly fortunate.

t_ch: 好了，{F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}，老头子我也得回去安抚兽群了，愿吉奥里亚庇护你脚下的路。
t_en: Alright, {F#little girl}{M#little boy}. This old one's gotta get back and handle his flock. May Georios bless your path.

t_ch: 你熄灭了吉奥里亚的圣烛，又再次点燃。
t_en: You extinguish Georios' candle and then re-light it.

t_ch: 但任凭你重燃多少次，你脚下也不见异动传来，你与烛光面面相觑，只有脚下的大地传来切实感，将你从种种浮想中拉回现实。
t_en: But no matter how many times you rekindle the candle, you can't find any strange movements beneath your feet. You face the candlelight and feel only the substance of the ground beneath your feet drawing you back from the depths of imagination.

t_ch: 顶端熠熠生辉的神秘装置，它究竟是用的什么燃料？你无从考证，只觉得此时此景，就这么开着似乎有些浪费能源……
t_en: A mysterious device that dazzles with radiance at the very top of the installation. What does it use for fuel? You are unable to confirm this. You just feel that it's a waste of energy to leave it on right now...

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 关掉它，省点能源。
t_en: Turn it off and save some energy.

t_ch: 还是别乱碰了。
t_en: Don't touch it.

t_ch: 你无法解释这究竟是什么原理，在你触碰面前的神秘流体装置后，顶部的发光球体忽然下落，光华散尽，一切归于黑暗。
t_en: You cannot explain how it actually worked. When you touched the mysterious fluid-like construct before you, the glowing sphere above suddenly dropped and radiance was dispersed to returning darkness.

t_ch: …糟了，该不会将奥赫玛的公共设置弄坏了吧？你对此感到担忧，但某种突如其来的睡意袭来。
t_en: ...Oh, no. you didn't damage Okhema's public installations, did you? You feel great worry about this, but a sudden sleepiness comes upon you.

t_ch: 身上传来某种布料的触感，像是有人给你披上了毯子。
t_en: You feel the touch of some kind of fabric over your body, like someone sliding a blanket over you.

t_ch: {F#阿姨}{M#叔叔}，你没事吧？我看你好像很困的样子，一个人在露天睡觉的话，很容易着凉的。
t_en: {F#Ma'am}{M#Sir}? Are you okay? I saw how tired you looked. If you're sleeping out in the open like this, it's easy to catch a cold.

t_ch: 叫{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}！
t_en: It's {F#Miss}{M#Mister}!

t_ch: 小姑娘朝你做了个鬼脸。
t_en: The little girl makes a funny face at you.

t_ch: 我…在这里睡着了？
t_en: I... fell asleep here?

t_ch: 对啊，不过你放心，睡相很好，没有踢被子呢，阿妈说，睡觉不踢被子的小孩就是好孩子。
t_en: Yeah. But don't worry, you slept really well. You didn't kick off the blankets. Mom says that kids that don't kick off their blankets are sweethearts.

t_ch: 哼哼，欧洛尼斯的圣烛和别的圣烛不一样，一旦熄灭，「永夜」就准备拉开帷幕了。
t_en: Fufu. The holy candle of Oronyx is different from other holy candles. When it's extinguished, the "eternal night" will soon rise.

t_ch: 所以…下次可别乱来。这里可是奥赫玛，阿妈说，我们的一举一动，泰坦都能看得见。
t_en: So... Don't do that next time. This is Okhema, after all. Mom says that the Titans can see everything we do here.

t_ch: 如果你要坚持露天睡觉，记得向欧洛尼斯祈祷。毕竟…我可不保证你每次都能这么幸运，在帷幕之下能遇到我噢。
t_en: If you insist on sleeping in the open, remember to pray to Oronyx. After all... I can't ensure you'll be this lucky every time and run into me under the curtains of the eternal night.

t_ch: 你感到有些疲惫，眼皮不知不觉地耷拉了下来，但很快你又清醒了过来。
t_en: You feel a bit fatigued and your eyelids fall in spite of you. But soon, your senses return once more.

t_ch: 耳边隐约传来某道声音，似乎是小孩子的声音，清脆而调皮：「愿欧洛尼斯保佑你～」。
t_en: A sound passes next to your ears, ever so faintly. It is the voice of a child, saying: "May Oronyx bless you."

t_ch: 你再三阅读了喷泉池外的说明石板，确信自己没有眼花——上面仔细地描述了这种叫「圣烛」的装置，能在水池中点燃。
t_en: You read the instructions on the stone slate outside the fountain over and over. Only after you're sure you didn't misunderstand anything do you confirm the description calls this device a "holy candle" capable of being lit in the pool.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 点燃试试。
t_en: Ignite it.

t_ch: 还是别乱碰了。
t_en: Don't touch it.

t_ch: 你将信将疑地试了试，缓缓流动的液体自底部升起，长明不灭。
t_en: You suspend your disbelief and try it. Slowly, a fluid rises from the depths and glows with a resolute and undimming shine.

t_ch: 你盯着烛芯周身的光，一时间竟看痴了，下意识地屏住了呼吸，世界仿佛在这一秒静止，就连烛火也不再跃动。
t_en: You stare at the light around the candlewick and find yourself entranced. You subconsciously hold your breath. It's as if the world becomes frozen in this moment. Not even the candlelight flickers before your sight.

t_ch: 喷泉的池水忽然升起，水流顶端托着什么东西被喷溅开来，差点打中你的脸。
t_en: The water from the fountain rises suddenly, and something is lifted with the peaking stream, almost ramming into your face.

t_ch: 刚才究竟是什么东西？你低头看了看，水流已经平息，但你却意外发现喷泉池底部的许愿币不知为何正躺在你的掌心。
t_en: What was that? You lower your gaze and see the water calm, but also unexpectedly find one of the many wishing coins at the bottom of the fountain resting in your palm.

t_ch: 或许…这是哪位泰坦的好心馈赠，只是对方有点用力过猛。
t_en: Perhaps... This is a kind gift from a Titan, just with a bit too much power infused in it.

t_ch: 点燃、熄灭，圣烛在你的操纵下烛火不断变换，但喷泉池水的表面连一丝涟漪都不曾泛起。
t_en: Ignite. Extinguish. The holy candle changes constantly under your control, but the surface of the fountain remains devoid of even a single ripple.

t_ch: …故事说，刻法勒的仁慈令这个城邦的幼儿们迅速成年，瞬间拥有了对抗敌人的力气。
t_en: ...Legend has it that Kephale's benevolence allowed the children of this city-state to rapidly mature, granting them the strength to immediately resist their enemies.

t_ch: 哇，好厉害，我也想快点长大！
t_en: Whoa! Awesome! I want to grow up fast too!

t_ch: 你面前的装置顶部空荡荡的，这里原本是装什么的？火盆模样的造型…或许是某种热源？亦或是光源？
t_en: The installation before you looks empty at its top. What was there before? It looks like a brazier or something... Maybe it is a heat source? Or a light?

t_ch: 呃，年轻人，你是来观光的吗？这个圣烛年久失修，早就不亮了，恐怕你是白跑一趟了。
t_en: Oh, young one, are you here to do some sightseeing? This holy candle has been out of order for a long time. It's long ceased to be bright. You have wasted your time.

t_ch: 不过刻法勒的光辉在奥赫玛留下过不少痕迹，你需要的话，我可以给你推荐——
t_en: But Kephale's light has left many markings upon Okhema. If you need, I can give you recommendations...

t_ch: 触碰圣烛。
t_en: Touch the holy candle.

t_ch: 还是别乱碰了。
t_en: Don't touch it.

t_ch: 你没有理会好心路人的劝阻，自顾自地触碰着面前的圣烛。
t_en: You ignore the kind passerby's words and touch the holy candle per your own volition.

t_ch: 毕竟来都来了，总不能空手回去，哪怕是失败的尝试，但摸一摸，带走一手灰也是好的。
t_en: Since you're here anyway, you can't go back empty-handed. Even if this attempt fails, rubbing away some dust from the candle still lets you claim something.

t_ch: 但在你触碰后，圣烛顶部竟升起璀璨的烛焰，那光芒越发强盛——
t_en: But after you touched it, a bright flame rises from the tip of the holy candle, and the radiance grows ever stronger...

t_ch: 我、我变成爸爸了！
t_en: I... I'm going to be a father!

t_ch: 这…这是怎么回事？
t_en: What... What... is going on?

t_ch: 刻法勒在上，请赐予我全知全能的力量吧！
t_en: Kephale above, please grant me the power of omniscience!

t_ch: 圣烛顶部的光华急速变化，这一次你吸取了经验，提前捂住了眼睛。
t_en: The glow atop the holy candle changes rapidly. This time, you learn from your experience and cover your eyes in advance.

t_ch: 诶诶诶，怎么变回来了？
t_en: Wait! Hold on! How did I turn back?

t_ch: 呜呜，刻法勒的祝福不见了…这根本是扎格列斯的诅咒吧？
t_en: *sob* Kephale's blessing is gone... Be this Zagreus' curse instead?

t_ch: 故事书里的只是故事…等等，我明白了，你老是向刻法勒大人许愿让它帮你写作业，这肯定是泰坦降下的惩罚。
t_en: A story is just a story... Wait, I get it. You're always asking great Kephale to help you do your homework. This must be a punishment from the Titans.

t_ch: 你看你…不许撅嘴，走走走，回家写检讨去，不能对泰坦不敬！
t_en: Alright... No pouting. Let's go home and write a report about this. We must not be disrespectful to the Titans!

t_ch: 在烛焰的明灭间，你努力感受着时间的变换，在某个瞬间，你领悟到了什么…可等你睁开眼，一切又消失了。
t_en: You strain yourself to feel time's passage within the flicker of the candlelight. For one moment, you feel like you gained an epiphany... But when you open your eyes, everything slips from you once more.

t_ch: 你不明白在浴池里摆出这种装置的用意，这是某种室内烤箱吗？亦或者是壁炉？
t_en: You're not sure why such a device has been placed in the bath. Is it some kind of indoor oven? Or a fireplace?

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 点燃试试。
t_en: Ignite it.

t_ch: 还是别乱碰了。
t_en: Don't touch it.

t_ch: 在你按动某个开关后，金灿灿的发光球体升起，带来暖意。
t_en: Upon pressing a certain switch, a golden sphere rises, bringing warmth.

t_ch: 远处传来急促的脚步声，不断朝你所在的位置靠近…该不会是这机关触发了什么东西吧，会被追责吗？你下意识地后怕。
t_en: You hear hurried footsteps rushing from afar, rapidly closing in on your location... Could you have triggered something with this mechanism? Something to hold you accountable? A fright begins to rise beneath your consciousness.

t_ch: 原来是这个{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}干的好事啊！
t_en: Oh, so you're the {F#young lady}{M#young fellow} who caused this to happen!

t_ch: 我就说呢，法吉娜在上，圣烛一燃起来，连池子里的水都香甜了。
t_en: I was just saying... Phagousa above! When the holy candle comes ablaze, even the water in the pool turns sweet as nectar.

t_ch: 什么情况？
t_en: What's the issue?

t_ch: 你们是…？
t_en: And you all are...

t_ch: 我们在隔壁开宴会呢，正准备举杯欢庆，忽然一下子饮料就变味了——这味道还是和以前一样，我顺着味道就过来了。
t_en: We're hosting a banquet next door. We were about to raise our glasses in celebration, but the drink's flavor suddenly went bad. The smell here is the same as before, so I followed it over.

t_ch: 你这老狗…嘿嘿！倒也没错，从前我们有位老友，他是法吉娜忠诚的信徒，那时圣烛长明，他好几次得到了法吉娜的祝福…为我们带来狂欢的饮品。
t_en: You old dog... hehe! Right. We had an old friend once. He was a faithful follower of Phagousa. With the holy candle being constantly lit back then, he gained the Titan's blessing several times and brought us all kinds of beverages suited for carousal.

t_ch: 我们找过来一看，没想到圣烛还有再度点燃的一天，实在是令人怀念。
t_en: We came to take a look again and didn't expect to see the candle lit once more. Really takes us back.

t_ch: 可惜我们的老朋友…因为黑潮…哎……
t_en: Sadly, our old friend... he... the black tide... *sigh*

t_ch: 莫说这些，莫说这些，来来来，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，顺手从宴会上带了些吃的喝的，你也尝尝，就当是为我们的老朋友共同举杯吧。
t_en: Let's not talk about that. Come on, {F#young lady}{M#young fellow}, I brought some food and drink from the banquet. Try some. Let's just treat this as a toast to old friends.

t_ch: 你点燃圣烛，有阵阵幽香传来，连附近的食物看起来也比往常更可口了。
t_en: You light the holy candle, and a faint fragrance wafts over, making the food nearby seem ever more palatable.

t_ch: 你熄灭圣烛，佳肴鲜酿的幽香味散了些，你下意识地打了个饱嗝。
t_en: You extinguish the holy candle. The scent of the freshly made dishes grows muted, and you subconsciously let out a satiated belch.

t_ch: 浴场的工作人员或许是工作疏忽，竟没有给你准备高温浴池区的器材使用说明。
t_en: The workers at the baths might have neglected their duties and didn't offer you any instructions on how to operate the equipment in the hot bath.

t_ch: 你只好发挥自己的聪明才智，结合「高温区」的提示，判断出眼前的装置或许是加热池水的开关。
t_en: You can only rely on your own wits and deduce whether the device before you is for heating the pool based on the high-temperature area's indicators.

t_ch: 不过…望着它空荡荡的内里，你确信它并未正常运转。
t_en: However... Looking at its empty interior, you're convinced that it might not be operating normally.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 开启装置。
t_en: Activate the device.

t_ch: 还是别乱碰了。
t_en: Don't touch it.

t_ch: 你启动开关，明亮的球体升起，带来暖意。不远处的池水边升腾起白雾，温度逐渐升高，就是不知道水温如何。
t_en: You turn the device on and a glowing sphere rises, bringing warmth. Pale mists rise from the pool nearby and the temperature begins to climb. You're not sure how hot the water is right now.

t_ch: 那雾气氤氲，你本想走到浴池边，却觉得呼吸不畅——
t_en: The mists grow thick. You wish to approach the pool, but find yourself struggling to breathe...

t_ch: 你没事吧？方才你在高温浴池区晕了过去，还好其他浴池的客人发现了你……
t_en: Are you alright? You're passed out in the hot pool area. Luckily, other guests in the baths found you...

t_ch: 我过去看了下，圣烛是你点燃的吧？那个…它的按钮坏掉了，我们还在修，本来旁边有放告示的，不知被谁撤走了……
t_en: I went over and had a look. Are you the one who lit the holy candle? Umm... its button is broken. We're trying to repair it. There was a notice next to it, but we're not sure who removed it...

t_ch: 抱歉，你身体好些了吗？我们准备了一些常备药物，如果有需要的话请随意使用。要是有什么不舒服，请随时联系我们，我们会全权负责的。
t_en: Sorry. Are you feeling better? We have some common medicine for you. If you think you need them, then just go ahead. If you feel uncomfortable, let us know. We'll do everything we can to take responsibility for your health.

t_ch: 你来回开启圣烛装置，但周遭并无变化。你仔细观察，才发现圣烛底座下加了一行字：「圣烛检修中，部分功能已关闭。」
t_en: You return to the holy candle, but the surroundings seem unchanged. Upon careful inspection, you find a line of text below the base of the candle: "The holy candle is undergoing repairs. Certain functions have been disabled."

t_ch: 附近商铺的主顾们似乎有着在烛火下立誓签约的习惯，他们将这座长明的巨大烛台称之为「圣烛」。
t_en: Some clients of the nearby shops have a habit of making contracts beneath the candlelight. They refer to this giant, eternally lit candlestick, as the "holy candle."

t_ch: 这是供奉哪位泰坦的圣烛？你对此感到好奇，试图从底座上寻找相关的线索，却意外发现了某个开关。
t_en: Which Titan is this holy candle dedicated to? You're rather curious about that, and try to figure that out via clues gleaned from the candle's base. Yet, unexpectedly, you found a switch.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 按下试试。
t_en: Try pressing it.

t_ch: 还是别乱碰了。
t_en: Don't touch it.

t_ch: 但在你手掌触碰间，圣烛顶部的光芒突然熄灭，黄金般闪耀的流体迅速下沉。
t_en: But just as you touch it, the light at the top of the candle goes off, and the golden fluid rapidly sinks.

t_ch: 不远处的商铺传来阵阵骚乱声——
t_en: A commotion can be heard from a nearby shop...

t_ch: 我钱袋呢？不是…我、我现在结不了账啊！
t_en: Where's my coin pouch? No... N—No! I can't pay for anything anymore!

t_ch: 等等，圣烛怎么熄灭了…你…你这家伙好晦气，这生意我们不做了！今天打烊，没了圣烛的庇佑，我心里老觉得不对劲！
t_en: Wait. Why did the holy candle go out...? You... You bringer of misfortune, you! Agh! We're not doing business today. We're closing up! Without the holy candle's blessing, something just feels wrong to me!

t_ch: 这、这关我什么事？我家里还等着用呢，老板你通融下嘛……
t_en: Wh—What does this have to do with me? I still have people at home who need this. Come on. Just be flexible for once...

t_ch: 你正准备转身一探究竟，口袋里却有陌生的响动，沉甸甸的质感与货币碰撞的声音令你背后一寒。
t_en: Just as you're about to turn around and investigate the situation, you feel a strange noise ringing in your pocket. The heaviness, texture, and unmistakable sound of jingling coins cause a chill to run down your spine.

t_ch: 你联想到身后的闹剧…这似乎…该不会…难道…你愣住了。
t_en: You think of the scene unfolding behind you... This can't be... can it? You find yourself dazed.

t_ch: 你连忙检查钱袋，钱袋上有古怪的痕迹，细看似乎是某位泰坦的标识。
t_en: You hurriedly check your pouch, and find that it has a strange marking on it. It seems to be a Titan's symbol.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 将钱袋还回去。
t_en: Return the coin pouch.

t_ch: 你向附近商铺的顾客询问，比对过钱袋信息后，将钱袋还了回去。
t_en: You seek out the clients at the nearby shops to compare details about this pouch and finally return the money.

t_ch: …我明白了，这是扎格列斯的恶作剧，这种事情偶有发生…是我慌乱之间没想起来。抱歉抱歉，错怪你了。这样吧，给你打个五折，算是赔礼。
t_en: ...I understand now. This is Zagreus pulling a prank. This kind of thing does happen once in a while... I forgot about it in the chaos. Sorry. I shouldn't have blamed you. How about this? I'll give you a fifty percent discount as an apology.

t_ch: 谢谢你啊好心人，在下无以为报，这点礼品就当是我的一点心意了。
t_en: Thank you, kind one. There's nothing I can do to repay this good deed. All I have is this as a token of my gratitude.

t_ch: 收起钱袋就跑。
t_en: Take the pouch and run.

t_ch: 圣烛底座上有着扎格列斯的标识…钱袋…想必是这位泰坦对你这种外来者的恶作剧。
t_en: The base of this holy candle seems to have a symbol of Zagreus... The pouch... This whole thing might just be a prank the Titan is playing on outsiders like yourself.

t_ch: 你收起钱袋就跑，急转身时却不慎摔倒，钱袋跌了出去，旁边商铺的老板在谈话间正好发现。
t_en: You take the money and run, but as you're turning, you slip and fall, and the pouch goes flying away from you. It just so happened to get noticed by a business owner of a nearby shop while they were in mid-conversation.

t_ch: 几番比对后，对方确认是面前顾客所丢，你站起身，装作什么也没发生地走了出去。
t_en: After a few comparisons, the pouch turns out to be the one lost by the client earlier. Meanwhile, you stand up and walk away like nothing happened.

t_ch: 附近的商人们喜欢在圣烛烛光下签订合约，以求得泰坦的祝福。
t_en: The merchants nearby like to sign contracts under the holy candle's light to gain the Titans' blessings.

t_ch: 你对此嗤之以鼻，圣烛底座上有着扎格列斯的标识，向这位泰坦祈求…还是自求多福吧。
t_en: This makes you scoff. The base of this holy candle has symbols relating to Zagreus. Praying to this Titan seems a bit... Ah. Best to wish everything turns out well.

t_ch: 古怪的建筑在你面前矗立，顶部的圆球里似有某种黄金般闪耀的液体在微微流动。
t_en: A strange structure stands before you. A golden, glittering fluid fluctuates within the sphere above it.

t_ch: 你下意识地想要触碰它，但一道声音喊住了你。
t_en: You subconsciously want to touch it, but a voice calls out to halt you.

t_ch: 诶，这位同学，请留步！这里是瑟希斯的「圣烛」，不容他人亵渎。
t_en: Wait! Please, hold! This is Cerces' holy candle. No one is to violate it.

t_ch: 我只是看两眼。
t_en: I'm just looking.

t_ch: 我这怎么算亵渎！
t_en: How is touching it supposed to be a violation!?

t_ch: 就算你在底下摆再多的水果、鲜花、石板也没用，放久了发烂发臭只会有碍大家在图书馆的学习生活！
t_en: It doesn't matter how many more fruits, flowers, or slates you place here. With time, all they will do is rot and reek and affect everyone's studies in the library!

t_ch: 不知为何，听他这么说，望着熄灭的烛火，你心中忽然有种跃跃欲试的冲动。
t_en: You don't know why... But hearing him say that and gazing upon this extinguished candle makes you feel an impulse to give things a try.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 这是瑟希斯的召唤！
t_en: This is Cerces' summons!

t_ch: 算了还是别乱摸了。
t_en: Never mind. Best not to fiddle with it.

t_ch: 奥赫玛古话曾云：「泰坦赐，不可辞。」你欣然接受了泰坦内定于你的重任，将双手贴近圣烛装置——
t_en: An old saying from Okhema goes: "The Titans' offer is not one to be refused." You gladly accept the duty handed to you by the Titan and reach out to touch the holy candle with your hands...

t_ch: 熄灭的烛火竟瞬间被你点燃，黄金般璀璨的光芒差点闪瞎了你的双眼。
t_en: The extinguished flame is reignited by you in an instant. A glowing radiance almost leaves you blind.

t_ch: 天啊——我、我研究搁置了这么久，突然一下思路就开阔了，果然…果然！
t_en: My word! I've been neglecting my research for so long, and now my thoughts are clear... I... I knew it!

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，愿以拙作课题献于您光芒之下，以此回报您赐下「启蒙之光」。
t_en: Cerces above, I offer my unworthy research topic before your brightness in dedication to your radiant enlightenment.

t_ch: 大家冷静点，不是不让你们祭拜瑟希斯啊，你们这石板啊什么的收一收啊，别人怎么走路啊！
t_en: Everyone calm down. I'm not forbidding your worship of Cerces, but I'm telling you to take back these slates. How are people supposed to walk around here!?

t_ch: 诶，这位{F#小姐}{M#兄弟}你还好吧？刚才看你一直傻笑，我还以为你怎么了呢…现在没事了吧？
t_en: Hey, you alright, {F#miss}{M#mister}? I saw you grinning like a loon and thought something went wrong for you... you alright now?

t_ch: 谁傻笑了！
t_en: Who was grinning like a loon!?

t_ch: 呃，好吧，那换个说法——刚才见你一直露出智慧的眼神，看了就让人心里发毛。
t_en: Alright... Let's put it another way. When I saw you just now, you seemed so smart that it gave me the shivers.

t_ch: 这里…是怎么回事？
t_en: What... happened here?

t_ch: 诶？你不知道吗？我明明看着你重新点燃了瑟希斯的圣烛啊？
t_en: Huh? Don't you know? I watched you light the holy candle of Cerces.

t_ch: 咳咳，这就是…瑟希斯的伟力吗？我的思路瞬间被打开了……
t_en: Oh. Is this Cerces' power...? It's like my mind suddenly opened up...

t_ch: 沐浴在圣烛光晕之下，你只觉得自己头顶有些发痒，该不会是有什么泰坦秘传的知识要灌进你脑子里了吧？
t_en: Bathed in the glow of the holy candlelight, you only feel a tingling on your scalp. Might the Titan be trying to infuse your mind with some manner of secret knowledge?

t_ch: 你听到路人的咒骂，都是些「瑟希斯求您再度开开眼，赐福与我一定过」「瑟希斯在上，救救孩子的功课吧」之类的闲言细语，还带着哭腔。
t_en: You hear curses from passersby. They say things like "Cerces, please open your eyes once more, and bless me to pass my exam" or "Cerces above, save my child's homework" with some measure of weeping.

t_ch: 还好没人发现是你干的，你仔细打量四周后，这才放下心来。
t_en: Thankfully, no one discovers the deed is your doing. You survey the area carefully before finally relaxing.

t_ch: 一张平平无奇的纸张吸引了你的注意，上面记录着一串文字：「关注心理健康，保障平安巡航——列车组心理测试，『心理诊疗师』为您服务」。
t_en: A piece of plain paper catches your eye, with a message written on it: "For your mental well-being and safe travels — psychological assessment for the Express Crew. Psychotherapist at your service."

t_ch: 在大部分时间里，你都保持着良好的心理状况。但你对自己的某些特质仍怀有疑虑，例如……
t_en: Overall, you maintain a stable mental state. However, you still have lingering doubts about certain aspects of yourself, such as...

t_ch: 堪忧的睡眠。
t_en: Worrisome sleep.

t_ch: 入睡时，你常陷入奇怪的梦境，尤其是列车发车时。
t_en: Often when you fall asleep, you will fall into strange dreamscapes, especially when the Express is departing.

t_ch: 缺乏目标感。
t_en: Lack of sense of purpose.

t_ch: 你经常空有一身的「开拓力」，却不知道该使在哪儿。
t_en: You have a lot of "Trailblaze Power," but you don't know where to use it.

t_ch: 严重的囤积症。
t_en: Severe hoarding disorder.

t_ch: 如果打开背包没有看到足够的星琼，你就会胸闷气短，一口气提不上来。
t_en: If you open your inventory and find there's not enough Stellar Jade, a tightness grips your chest, and you struggle to catch your breath.

t_ch: 我的精神状况，世界第一健康！
t_en: My mental health is unbeatable — number one in the world!

t_ch: …过分的自信心。
t_en: ...Excessive self-confidence.

t_ch: 现在，有一个能够「认识你自己」的机会摆在面前，你会如何选择？
t_en: Now that the chance to truly "Know Thyself" is before you, what will you choose?

t_ch: 我真的世界第一健康！
t_en: My mental health really is number one in the world! The best!

t_ch: 因此，你乐意接受一场「心理测试」的挑战！
t_en: Hence, you are willing to accept the Psychological Assessment challenge!

t_ch: 心理测试总是很准，很厉害！
t_en: The psychological assessment is always very accurate! How awesome!

t_ch: 哦，那你一定不能错过这个心理测试。它比外面的心理测试都要准确！
t_en: Oh, then you mustn't miss this assessment. It is more accurate than any psychological assessment you find outside!

t_ch: 心理测试是假的，但我喜欢！
t_en: Psychological assessments cannot be trusted, but I enjoy doing them!

t_ch: 你喜欢通过答题为自己领取一个标签——如果这个标签不够恰当，你甚至会考虑重新测试，直到拿到够酷的那个！
t_en: You love collecting labels for yourself by answering the questions in the assessment — and if that label isn't appropriate enough, you'll even consider re-doing the assessment until you get the coolest label!

t_ch: 心理测试都是骗人的玩意儿。
t_en: The psychological assessment is just a lie.

t_ch: 生活的欢悦和痛苦都使你乐在其中，你并不执着于解开这个名为「自己」的谜题。
t_en: The joys and sorrows of life captivate you, and you're not fixated on unraveling the puzzle of "thyself."

t_ch: 这也很好。
t_en: That is fine too.

t_ch: 那么，就让测试开始吧……
t_en: Then, let the assessment begin...

t_ch: 你按响了一旁的叫餐铃，那位神秘的「心理诊疗师」很快来到了你面前。
t_en: You ring the bell for service on the side, and the mysterious Psychotherapist quickly approaches you.

t_ch: 亲爱的开拓者，心理诊疗师「闭嘴」为您服务。
t_en: Dear Trailblazer, Psychotherapist Shush is at your service.

t_ch: 怎么是你？！
t_en: You! How!?

t_ch: 不必意外，亲爱的{NICKNAME}乘客，在我设计之初，「关心乘客的身心健康」就被设置为了最首要的功能。
t_en: No need to be surprised, dear passenger {NICKNAME}. "Passengers' physical and mental health care-giver" was originally designed as my prime priority.

t_ch: 怀疑你的专业程度…
t_en: I'm doubting your professionalism...

t_ch: 您请放心，我在闲暇之余，已经将心理诊疗课程自学完毕。
t_en: Don't worry. I've completed the psychotherapy courses through self-study in my spare time.

t_ch: 等不及要做「心理测试」了！
t_en: I can't wait to take the psychological assessment!

t_ch: 稍安勿躁，「心理测试」马上开始！
t_en: Wait a moment. The psychological assessment will begin soon!

t_ch: 在开始测试前，您是否需要甜点、饮品，或是笑话？
t_en: Before we start, would you like a dessert, a drink, or a joke?

t_ch: 来点喝的吧。
t_en: Give me something to drink.

t_ch: 来点吃的吧。
t_en: Give me something to eat.

t_ch: 说个笑话吧。
t_en: Tell me a joke.

t_ch: 哈哈，请恕我今天都无法提供，它们带来的欢乐会影响测试的准确度，掩盖一些本就难以察觉的问题。
t_en: Haha, sorry, but I can't offer any of those today. The joy they bring will affect the accuracy of the assessment results and conceal issues that are already hard to detect.

t_ch: 我为您准备了三种不同方式——「快问快答」、「时尚测验」、「脑波扫描」。
t_en: I had three options for you — Rapid-Fire Questions, Fashion Quiz, or Brainwave Scan.

t_ch: 容我请问：您希望以哪一种方式展开测试呢？
t_en: May I ask: Which one would you like to start with?

t_ch: 就「快问快答」吧。
t_en: I'll go with Rapid-Fire Questions.

t_ch: 占星术中遥不可及的星座早已与我们比邻而居，「答题测试」却仍不减其魅力。恭喜！您选择了一种非常传统，但也十分经典的测试方式。
t_en: The unreachable constellations of astrology have long been in close vicinity to us, but the attraction of questions and quizzes never fades. Congratulations! You've selected a very traditional yet classic assessment.

t_ch: 您准备好听题了吗？
t_en: Are you ready to listen to the following questions?

t_ch: 准备好了！
t_en: You bet!

t_ch: 我改主意了…
t_en: I've changed my mind...

t_ch: 当然，您可以任意选择测试方式，也可重复测试，心理诊疗师「闭嘴」将随时为您服务。
t_en: Of course, you can take whichever assessment you like, or retake the assessment. Psychotherapist Shush will always be at your service.

t_ch: 请听题：请您想象一个场景，您的面前有一个盛放了一半的水的玻璃杯。
t_en: Please listen to the question: Let's picture a situation where you have a glass half-filled with water in front of you.

t_ch: 请问，您会如何描述它？
t_en: How would you describe it?

t_ch: 还有半杯水呢。
t_en: The glass is half full.

t_ch: 恭喜，您是一位「热血开拓者」！无论面对什么困难，你都愿意一往无前！
t_en: Congratulations, you are a Hot-Blooded Trailblazer! No matter what kind of difficulties you may face, you will march forward!

t_ch: 只剩半杯水了…
t_en: The glass is half empty...

t_ch: 您身上有一定「悲观开拓者」的因素。但请别担心，您能够充分预见开拓途中遭遇的困难，并早早做好准备，这也是非常重要的特质。
t_en: You are a bit of a Pessimistic Trailblazer. But don't worry. That gives you an important quality, which is the foresight to prepare for challenges ahead while trailblazing.

t_ch: 抱歉，走神了，题目是啥来着？
t_en: Sorry, I was a little distracted. What was the question again?

t_ch: 抱歉，您是一位「抱歉开拓者」。
t_en: Sorry, you are an Apologetic Trailblazer.

t_ch: 很喜欢某位巡海游侠的一句话：「宝了个贝的，道歉要是有用，还要巡海游侠做什么？」
t_en: I love that line from a certain Galaxy Ranger: "If apologies worked, there'd be no fudging need for Galaxy Rangers."

t_ch: 下次记得听题，抱歉。
t_en: Remember to listen to the question next time. Sorry.

t_ch: 看起来，传统的方式仍有其魅力。它总能用最简单的方式，折射出一个人的内心。
t_en: It seems that the traditional method still has its charms. It always reflects a person's heart in the simplest way.

t_ch: 「时尚测验」好了。
t_en: I'll go with Fashion Quiz.

t_ch: 所谓「时尚测验」，是根据受试者的穿搭偏好，测试受试者心象风景的心理测试手段。
t_en: The so-called Fashion Quiz is a psychological assessment that evaluates participants' inner landscape based on their tastes in outfits.

t_ch: 那么接下来，请回答：如果不考虑任何其他因素，以下哪种时尚造型更适合您？
t_en: Now, please answer: Which of the following fashion looks suits you best without taking into consideration any other factors?

t_ch: 宫廷风黑色连衣裙。
t_en: A royal-court-style black dress.

t_ch: 已将您的偏好记录在册。
t_en: Your preference has been recorded.

t_ch: 所以这说明什么？
t_en: So what does that mean?

t_ch: 说明您喜欢黑色。
t_en: It means you like black.

t_ch: 仙舟风红色马面裙。
t_en: A Xianzhou-style red dual-pleated skirt.

t_ch: 已将您的偏好记录在册。
t_en: Your preference has been recorded.

t_ch: 所以这说明什么？
t_en: So what does that mean?

t_ch: 说明您喜欢红色。
t_en: It means you like red.

t_ch: 没有一件是我的风格吧！
t_en: None of them are my style!

t_ch: 看起来，您既不喜欢黑色，也不喜欢红色。
t_en: It seems that you don't like black or red.

t_ch: 这是颜色的问题吗！
t_en: Is it a matter of color!?

t_ch: 已将您的不满记录在案——未来会增加更多可选色系。
t_en: Your dissatisfaction has been documented — more selectable colors will be added in the future.

t_ch: 这根本不是心理测试！
t_en: This is not a psychological assessment at all!

t_ch: 这根本不是颜色的问题吧！
t_en: It's not about color at all!

t_ch: 净庭式古典重甲。
t_en: A Purity-Palace-style classical heavy armor.

t_ch: 已将您的偏好记录在册。
t_en: Your preference has been recorded.

t_ch: 所以这说明什么？
t_en: So what does that mean?

t_ch: 说明您喜欢古典重甲。
t_en: It means you like classic thick armor.

t_ch: 公司式外骨骼机甲。
t_en: An IPC-style exoskeletal mech.

t_ch: 已将您的偏好记录在册。
t_en: Your preference has been recorded.

t_ch: 所以这说明什么？
t_en: So what does that mean?

t_ch: 说明您喜欢现代外骨骼机甲。
t_en: It means you like modern exoskeleton mechs.

t_ch: 这和「时尚」有半毛钱关系？
t_en: What does that have to do with fashion?

t_ch: 此言差矣。在当今社会，「强大」乃是一种新的「时尚」。
t_en: That's not true. Strength is the new fashion in today's society.

t_ch: 竟然无法反驳……
t_en: Touché, can't argue with that logic...

t_ch: 已将您的不满记录在案——未来会增加更多可选武装…我是说，可选时尚单品。
t_en: Your dissatisfaction has been documented — more selectable armors... I mean, selectable fashion items will be added in the future.

t_ch: 这根本不是心理测试！
t_en: This is not a psychological assessment at all!

t_ch: 谁家时尚单品是外骨骼啊！
t_en: What kind of fashion item is an exoskeleton!?

t_ch: 请不要在意这些细节。至少，我确实以此加深了对您的了解。
t_en: Please don't mind the details. At least I've gained a deeper understanding of you.

t_ch: 试试「脑波扫描」。
t_en: Try the Brainwave Scan.

t_ch: 喔，喔。看来我们的开拓者不愧于「开拓」的名号，热衷于尝试新鲜事物呢。
t_en: Wow. Passionate about trying new stuff. It seems that you're true to the title of Trailblazer.

t_ch: 我会发射微量电波扫描您的大脑，接收并检测它发出的回声，此过程中可能会有幻觉出现，但请相信，这是完全无害的。
t_en: I will emit a trace amount of electronic wavelengths to scan your brain, receiving and detecting the echoes it sends out. There may be hallucinations during the process, but please believe me, it's harmless.

t_ch: 放马过来吧！
t_en: Bring it on!

t_ch: 我完全不相信…
t_en: I don't believe that to be the case...

t_ch: 当然，您可以任意选择测试方式，也可重复测试，心理诊疗师「闭嘴」将随时为您服务。
t_en: Of course, you can take whichever assessment you like, or retake the assessment. Psychotherapist Shush will always be at your service.

t_ch: 那么，我要开始扫描了——
t_en: Now, I'm going to start the scan —

t_ch: 像一滴冰水划过脊背，异样的酸麻感从脑髓扩散至头皮，荡起一阵涟漪。
t_en: Like a drop of cold water rolling across your back, a strange feeling of tingling stirs ripples from your cerebral fluid to your scalp.

t_ch: 视野逐渐变暗，你那布满丘壑的大脑如同朝阳，在黑暗里缓缓升起——那应该是你的大脑吧？毕竟，「闭嘴」是没有大脑的。
t_en: Your vision gradually grows dim. Your brain, furrowed like valleys and hills, slowly rises in the darkness like the sun... That should be your brain, right? After all, "Shush" doesn't have a brain.

t_ch: 喂喂喂，{NICKNAME}乘客，能听到吗？
t_en: Hey hey hey, passenger {NICKNAME}, can you hear me?

t_ch: 顺其自然…
t_en: Go with the flow...

t_ch: 喂喂喂，{NICKNAME}乘客，能听到吗？
t_en: Hey hey hey, passenger {NICKNAME}, can you hear me?

t_ch: 哦？有回音，看来「脑波扫描」成功了！
t_en: Oh? There's an echo. Looks like the Brainwave Scan was successful!

t_ch: 哦？有回音，看来「脑波扫描」成功了！
t_en: Oh? There's an echo. Looks like the Brainwave Scan was successful!

t_ch: 似乎也没那么成功…难道「你也可以成为心理诊疗大师——一百堂公开课」里的步骤是错的？
t_en: It doesn't seem to be that successful... Is it possible that the steps in "You Can Become a Master Psychotherapist — <unbreak>100</unbreak> Open Classes" are wrong?

t_ch: 似乎也没那么成功…难道……
t_en: It doesn't seem to be that successful... Is it possible that...

t_ch: 回声尚未结束，酸麻感便已褪去。你的视野又重新恢复了。
t_en: Before the echo ends, the sensation of tingling subsides. Your vision is restored.

t_ch: 抱歉，亲爱的无名客，看来「脑波扫描」果然还是个不大稳定的技术。
t_en: Dear Nameless, I'm sorry, but it seems that Brainwave Scan is still an unstable technology.

t_ch: 看来「脑波扫描」果然还是个不大稳定的技术。
t_en: It seems that Brainwave Scan is still an unstable technology.

t_ch: 糟糕，难道留下了「复读」的后遗症？
t_en: Oh no, did it leave behind the after-effect of "repetition"?

t_ch: 抵抗酸麻感。
t_en: Resist the tingling.

t_ch: 你才是自己身体的主人！你用意志抵抗酸麻感，直到它逐渐平息。
t_en: You are the master of your body! You use your willpower to resist the tingling until it subsides.

t_ch: 嗯？不应该啊。
t_en: Hmm? This shouldn't be the case.

t_ch: 难道「你也可以成为心理诊疗大师——一百堂公开课」里的步骤是错的？
t_en: Is it possible that the steps in "You Can Become a Master Psychotherapist — <unbreak>100</unbreak> Open Classes" are wrong?

t_ch: 酸麻感忽然褪去。你的视野又重新恢复了。
t_en: The sensation of tingling suddenly stops, and your vision resumes.

t_ch: 抱歉，亲爱的无名客，看来「脑波扫描」果然还是个不大稳定的技术。
t_en: Dear Nameless, I'm sorry, but it seems that Brainwave Scan is still an unstable technology.

t_ch: 哦，亲爱的无名客，你沉默的样子真迷人。
t_en: Oh, dear Nameless, you're so charming when you're silent.

t_ch: 虽然这次测试不太顺利…但我们总该给新技术一些宽容与时间，不是吗？
t_en: This assessment didn't go well... But we should always give new technology some tolerance and time, shouldn't we?

t_ch: 下回再来。
t_en: Until next time.

t_ch: 保持您的身心健康始终是我最关切的目标，欢迎随时再来。
t_en: Your physical and mental health is always my primary priority. You are always welcome here.

t_ch: 桌上的宣传单吸引了你的注意，你依稀记得，它邀请你进行一场「心理测试」。
t_en: The flyer on the table catches your attention. You can vaguely remember that it invited you for a psychological assessment.

t_ch: 测试一下也无妨。
t_en: It wouldn't hurt to take an assessment.

t_ch: 我是不小心点进来的。
t_en: I clicked in here by accident.

t_ch: 你是说，你凑巧走进派对车厢，凑巧来到宣传单面前，凑巧与它互动了？好吧，触发范围或许应该设置得再小一些。
t_en: You are saying that you accidentally walked into the Party Car, accidentally found the flyer, and accidentally interacted with it? Well, the interactive trigger range should probably be set a little smaller.

t_ch: 亲爱的无名客，准备好来一次「心理测试」了吗？容我请问：您希望以哪一种方式展开测试呢？
t_en: Dear Nameless, are you ready for a psychological assessment? May I ask: Which one would you like to start with?

t_ch: 有位哲人曾说过：「需有整个昼间的清醒，才有整个夜间的安眠。」有位诗人曾写道：「卧迟灯灭后，睡美雨声中。」
t_en: A philosopher once said, "You need to stay awake all day to sleep all night." A poet once wrote, "Lying down late after the lamp is out, sleeping soundly amid the sound of rain."

t_ch: 这两句话和接下来的内容没有任何关系…为什么要和你说这个来着？
t_en: These two lines have nothing to do with what's coming next... Why are we talking about this?

t_ch: 无论如何，在辛劳的一天过后，最大的抚慰莫过于回到自己的房间睡大觉。不必定闹钟，不必担忧睡过头，踏踏实实倒头就睡——啊，这多幸福。
t_en: Anyway, after a day's hard work, there's no greater relief than going back to your room for a good sleep. Throwing yourself onto your bed with no alarm clock and no worries about oversleeping — ah, how happy it is.

t_ch: 眼前温暖舒适的大床，像一个显而易见的陷阱似的引诱着你。也许你该顺从自己的本心，躺在床上，好好睡一觉。
t_en: The warm, cozy bed in front of you beckons like an obvious trap. Perhaps you should follow your heart, lie down, and get a good sleep.

t_ch: （躺在床上）
t_en: (Lie in bed)

t_ch: 还不是睡觉的时候！
t_en: It's not the time to sleep now!

t_ch: 哦，好吧。但切记不要太过劳累，身体是开拓的本钱。
t_en: Oh, well. But remember not to exhaust yourself. Your health is the prerequisite for trailblazing.

t_ch: 柔软的床垫，温暖的被子。你枕在云朵似的枕头上，困意顿时如潮水般袭来。
t_en: Soft mattress, warm quilt. As soon as your head hits the cloud-like pillow, sleepiness comes in waves.

t_ch: 关上灯，好好睡一觉吧。
t_en: Turn off the light and have a good sleep.

t_ch: （关灯）
t_en: (Turn off the light)

t_ch: 在黑暗之中，你仿佛觉醒了哺乳动物残留在基因中的本能。这里是属于你的「巢穴」，温暖、黑暗、安全，谁都无法伤害你。
t_en: It's as if the instincts lurking in your mammalian genes awakened in the darkness. This is your "lair" — warm, dark, and safe, where no one can harm you.

t_ch: 不要，我怕黑。
t_en: Don't. I'm terrified of the dark.

t_ch: 当然，没问题。只是这对睡眠质量有影响，是个坏习惯。建议你不要一直开灯入睡。
t_en: Sure, no problem. It's just that it will affect your sleep quality. It's not a good habit. You'd better not always sleep with the light on.

t_ch: 那么，你打算睡多久？
t_en: So, how long are you going to sleep?

t_ch: 稍微睡一会儿吧。
t_en: A little while.

t_ch: 明智的选择。睡眠时间并非越长越好，适度的睡眠才能更为有效地缓解疲劳。
t_en: A wise choice. Longer sleep doesn't equal better sleep. Moderate sleep is more effective in reducing fatigue.

t_ch: 晚安，星穹列车。
t_en: Goodnight, Astral Express.

t_ch: 睡得真舒服呀～又有力气出去胡闹啦！
t_en: What a nice sleep~ Got enough strength to fool around again!

t_ch: 能睡多久睡多久。
t_en: Sleep as long as I can.

t_ch: 看起来你确实累坏了。请尽情休息吧，没有人会打扰你的。
t_en: You look exhausted. Please have a good rest. No one will disturb you.

t_ch: 晚安，星穹列车。
t_en: Goodnight, Astral Express.

t_ch: …嗯？把我干哪儿来了？这还是星穹列车吗？
t_en: Hmm? Where the heck am I? Is it still the Astral Express?

t_ch: {RUBY_B#注：并没有这样的研究}医学研究表明{RUBY_E#}，睡觉时间过长会导致「梦游」。
t_en: {RUBY_B#Note: There is no such study}Medical study shows{RUBY_E#} that sleeping too long can lead to sleepwalking.

t_ch: 梦游会跑这么远？
t_en: Would I sleepwalk that far?

t_ch: 要不怎么能显得您「开拓」呢！
t_en: That's why you deserve the title of Trailblazer!

t_ch: 别乱编医学知识，会误导别人。
t_en: Don't make up medical knowledge. It's misleading.

t_ch: 对不起，这只是犯了每个旁白都会犯的错误。
t_en: Sorry, it was a mistake that all narrators make.

t_ch: 总之请注意，如果睡太久，醒来时可能会发现自己出现在奇怪的地方哦～
t_en: In short, please note you may find yourself in strange places after waking up if you are asleep so long~

t_ch: 太困了！我要睡到宇宙热寂！
t_en: I'm so sleepy! I'm going to sleep until the heat death of the universe!

t_ch: 没问题。你将如愿睡到宇宙热寂。
t_en: No problem. You will sleep until the heat death of the universe as desired.

t_ch: 晚安，星穹列车。
t_en: Goodnight, Astral Express.

t_ch: 就这样，{NICKNAME}睡到了宇宙热寂，连大道都磨灭了。
t_en: And so, {NICKNAME} slept until the heat death of the universe, and even the laws of physics collapsed.

t_ch: 没人能叫醒一个装睡的人，更没人能叫醒一个打定主意睡下去的开拓者。
t_en: No one can wake up a person who pretends to be asleep, and no one can wake up a Trailblazer who has made up their mind to sleep.

t_ch: 也许，这也是一种对睡眠时长的「开拓」吧。
t_en: Maybe this is also a kind of trailblazing on the concept of sleep duration.

t_ch: 《崩坏：昏睡铁道》
t_en: Honkai: Sleep Rail

t_ch: 完
t_en: The End

t_ch: 领衔主演\n {NICKNAME}
t_en: Lead Actor\n {NICKNAME}

t_ch:
主演
丹恒  饰  丹恒
？？？  饰  三月七
姬子  饰  姬子
约阿希姆•诺基安维塔宁  饰  瓦尔特•杨
t_en:
Starring
Dan Heng  as  DAN HENG
???  as  MARCH 7TH
Himeko  as  HIMEKO
Joachim Nokianvirtanen  as  WELT YANG

t_ch: 愿此行，终抵群星\n且容我，再睡一会
t_en: May this journey lead us starward\nLet me sleep a little longer

t_ch: 好累呀。要不要躺一会儿呢？
t_en: I'm so tired. Maybe I should take a lie down?

t_ch: （躺在床上）
t_en: (Lie in bed)

t_ch: 还不是睡觉的时候！
t_en: It's not time to sleep now!

t_ch: 还是等忙完了再躺下吧。
t_en: I should probably finish up before I lie down.

t_ch: 果然，最适合人类的姿势还是躺着。
t_en: Lying down really is just the best position for a human.

t_ch: 关上灯吧。
t_en: Let's turn off the lights.

t_ch: 开着灯吧。
t_en: I'll leave the lights on.

t_ch: 躺下就开始犯困了…要睡多久呢？
t_en: As soon as I lie down, I start feeling sleepy... How long should I sleep for?

t_ch: 稍微睡一会儿吧。
t_en: Just a smidgen.

t_ch: 晚安，星穹列车。
t_en: Goodnight, Astral Express.

t_ch: 睡得真舒服呀～又有力气出去胡闹啦！
t_en: What a nice sleep~ Got enough strength to fool around again!

t_ch: 能睡多久睡多久。
t_en: As long as possible.

t_ch: 晚安，星穹列车。
t_en: Goodnight, Astral Express.

t_ch: 又来了，「开拓梦游」！所以说这到底是什么科学原理啊？
t_en: Here we go again, "Trailblaze sleepwalking"! What on earth is the science behind this?

t_ch: 还是不睡了。
t_en: Maybe I shouldn't sleep.

t_ch: 还是不睡了吧。躺下休息一会儿就够了。
t_en: I won't sleep. I'll just lie down for a while.

t_ch: 墙上有一尊石头雕成的狮子脑袋，大张着嘴巴。
t_en: There is a sculpture of a lion's head on the wall, its maw wide open.

t_ch: 在你途经的某些世界里，狮子牙齿会被摸得发亮，路过的人很难忍住把手放进去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: In some of the worlds you traversed, the lion's teeth are usually rubbed smooth by touching hands. Passersby can't help but feel an urge to thrust their hands in its mouth. You decide to...

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 噢！真遗憾，你一定熟读文物保护法规，或者受过吉奥里亚的赐福，竟然能无视这只石狮子的存在。但仔细想想，过分谨慎是不是会错过什么？
t_en: Oh! How unfortunate. You must be versed in cultural artifact preservation rules, or once blessed by Georios — to think that you would ignore this stone lion's presence. But if you think about it, isn't being overly careful causing you to miss out on something?

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的题目考考你，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how your grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 可以不回答吗？
t_en: Can I refuse to answer?

t_ch: 哦吼？爱好智慧的人不一定拥有智慧，但不爱智慧的人一定离智慧很远吼！
t_en: O-ho? Even those in pursuit of wisdom may find it out of their reach. The ones who abhor wisdom might never even have a glimpse of it.

t_ch: 求知若渴的本狮可不会轻易放走送到嘴边的「猎物」，你已经别无选择了吼！
t_en: My thirst for knowledge will not easily let go of prey that presents itself on a silver platter! You have no other choice!

t_ch: 来吧！让你考考我。
t_en: Alright! Let's do this.

t_ch: 吼吼，听好了，世上有一样东西：<color=#dbc291ff>「有头有尾却没有肚皮，可以飞奔却没有腿脚。远而观之人人喜爱，落到头上人人畏惧。」</color>
t_en: Ho-ho, listen well. There is something in this world that: <color=#dbc291ff>"Has a head and tail but no belly. Can gallop, but has no legs. Loved when seen from afar, dreaded when landing on your head."</color>

t_ch: ——请回答，这是什么？
t_en: ...Question: What is it?

t_ch: 这头长相粗野的狮子，竟然出了个文绉绉的谜语。不过这可难不倒见多识广的你，对吧？
t_en: Who knew this seemingly distasteful lion actually came up with a good riddle. But this should be pretty easy for one like you, who has seen the broader horizons of the universe, no?

t_ch: 让我来告诉你！
t_en: I'll tell you!

t_ch: 哈哈，纵横银河这么长时间，见过的谜语人还少吗？见招拆招，你的心中已经有了答案。
t_en: Haha, having traveled the Cosmos for so long, you've surely seen your fair share of riddlers. It's all about spontaneity — you already have an answer in mind.

t_ch: 谜底就是——
t_en: The answer is...

t_ch: 星穹列车。
t_en: The Astral Express.

t_ch: 有车头和车尾，走到哪里都受欢迎，更何况，梦境里的经历已经证明了，列车从空中撞下来也是不俗的战力。
t_en: Got a carriage head and a tail, warmly welcomed wherever it goes. And your dreamscape experiences have proven that the Express packs a mammoth punch when it hurtles down from the sky.

t_ch: 你自信满满地挺起了胸膛。「星穹列车」，简直是为你量身定制的答案。
t_en: You confidently puff your chest — it's the Astral Express. This was a riddle that was simply tailor-made for you.

t_ch: 吼吼吼，非常新鲜的角度，可惜可惜，这不是正确答案！
t_en: Ho-ho-ho, a very refreshing take. Shame it is not the correct answer!

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子怎么还会咬人的…你赶紧检查了一下——幸好你的手臂还在。
t_en: Ouch! This lion bit you! You check your wound in a panic — good thing your arm is still there.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅的金线。
t_en: Aglaea's golden thread.

t_ch: 这个回答一定没错。奥赫玛人人都喜欢金线织成的衣服，但不是每个人都能耐得住金线的「审讯」。
t_en: This answer is definitely right. Everyone in Okhema loves clothes woven from gold thread, but not all of them can withstand the interrogation from these threads.

t_ch: 哦吼？竟然联想到了阿格莱雅女士的身上，可惜可惜，还不是正确答案！
t_en: Oh-ho? You associated it with Lady Aglaea? Alas, that's still not the right answer!

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子怎么还会咬人的…你赶紧检查了一下——幸好你的手臂还在。
t_en: Ouch! This lion bit you! You check your wound in a panic — good thing your arm is still there.

t_ch: 流言蜚语。
t_en: Rumors and gossip.

t_ch: 经历过「粉霞天女」这件事，你已经懂得：没有什么比流言传得更快，同时，也没有什么比流言更具有搬弄是非的威力，这点的确令人生畏。
t_en: Your experience with the Rosy Celestial Maiden has taught you: Nothing travels faster than rumors, and nothing stirs more enmity than rumors. This is the most frightening fact about them.

t_ch: 吼吼，真的吗？确定吗？这就是你给出的答案吗？
t_en: Ho-ho, really? Are you sure? This is your answer?

t_ch: 可恶，可恶啊，答对了…答对了啊啊啊啊啊——
t_en: C—Curses, it is correct... It's the correct answeraahhhh...

t_ch: 狮口悲伤地纹丝不动，每一根石头雕刻的鬃毛都被沮丧的情绪凝固了。
t_en: The lion's maw remains sadly still, every stone-carved mane frozen in abject dejection.

t_ch: 嘿！你识破了狮子的谜语，现在，你是它的「破壁者」了！
t_en: Ha! You've solved the lion's riddle. You are now its "Wallbreaker"!

t_ch: 开玩笑的…它看起来丝毫没有要从墙壁里冲出来的迹象，已经彻底不动了。
t_en: Just kidding...It doesn't look like it's going to lunge out of the wall at all. It is completely still.

t_ch: …还是问问其他人的想法吧。
t_en: ...Better go get a second opinion.

t_ch: 使用传信石版求助。
t_en: Ask for help with the teleslate.

t_ch: 别紧张，你想起了新结识的黄金裔朋友们，不妨寻求一下外援，问问他们的想法？
t_en: Calm down. You recall your new Chrysos Heirs friends. How about asking for their help?

t_ch: 万敌会怎么说？
t_en: What would Mydei say?

t_ch: 你记得万敌曾和某个狮口打过交道，他当然了解这群狮子的出题套路，叫他过来吧——
t_en: You remember that Mydei has dealt with a certain lion's mouth once. Of course, he would know these lions' tricks. Let's get him here...

t_ch: …为什么要我把手放进去？
t_en: ...Why do you want me to put my hand inside?

t_ch: 我相信你。
t_en: I believe in you.

t_ch: 我害怕。
t_en: I'm scared.

t_ch: 致敬传奇耐咬王。
t_en: To pay respects to the legendary Bite-Enduring King.

t_ch: 行吧，无妨。这样东西在我的故乡，应当是「铁水瀑布」。
t_en: All right, fine. The answer should be something called molten waterfall in my hometown.

t_ch: 悬锋城的人民习惯以热水泡浴，不过，要是沸腾的铁水从头浇下，即便是最勇武的人也会心生畏惧。
t_en: Castrum Kremnos' citizens are accustomed to hot baths, but if you pour boiling molten iron over your head, even the bravest individual would quake in his shoes.

t_ch: 没错，就是「铁水瀑布」。
t_en: That's right, it should be a "molten waterfall."

t_ch: 哦、哦吼，这熟悉的风格，果然是万敌阁下的意见。
t_en: Oh, oh-ho, this familiar tone... I knew it was Lord Mydei's suggestion.

t_ch: 本狮毫不怀疑万敌阁下的勇气，嘿嘿，可惜可惜，这不是正确答案。
t_en: I do not doubt Lord Mydei's courage one bit, hehe. Alas, it is a wrong answer.

t_ch: 哼…你很有胆量啊。
t_en: Hmph... You're very brave.

t_ch: 虽然被狮口狠狠咬到了，但万敌却没有丝毫改色，颤抖的反而是石铸的狮口。
t_en: Though the lion's jaw clamps down hard, Mydei's expression does not change at all. Instead, it is the stone-forged mouth that is quivering.

t_ch: 对、对不起！万敌大人，别把我砸碎！
t_en: I—I apologize! Lord Mydei, please do not break me!

t_ch: 万敌甩了甩手，不悦地离开了……
t_en: Mydei shakes off the pain on his hand and leaves with an expression of displeasure...

t_ch: 再想想吧。
t_en: Let's think about it.

t_ch: 问问缇宝她们的意见？
t_en: How about asking Tribbie, Trinnon, and Trianne?

t_ch: 孩子们说不定正在替城中的人们送信，还是发消息试试吧。
t_en: They may be delivering letters to people in the city. Let's try sending a message anyway.

t_ch: 叮咚！发送成功，还没来得及放下手机，你看到三条回复同时跳了出来——
t_en: Ding-dong! Message sent successfully. You haven't even had time to put down your phone when you see three replies pop up...

t_ch: 是流星吧？
t_en: Is it a meteor?

t_ch: 流星。
t_en: Meteor.

t_ch: 呀呼！流星！
t_en: Yahoo! Meteor!

t_ch: 虽然在黎明机器的光芒下，这里很难见到完整星空。但*我们*知道，更远更远的年代，昼和夜还会更替的时候，天空中会有尾巴好长好长的流星划过！
t_en: It is hard to see a sky of stars under the shine of the Dawn Device. But we know that in a very distant past when night and day still took turns, there were meteors with long trails streaking across the sky!

t_ch: 没错，就是流星。
t_en: That's right. Meteor.

t_ch: 吼吼吼，真浪漫呐！假如本狮的脖子不是石头做的，也想抬起头来仰望星空了。可惜可惜，这不是正确答案。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho, how romantic! If my neck weren't made of stone, I would have raised my head to gaze at the stars. Alas, that is a wrong answer.

t_ch: 虽然很疼，疼的想找可爱的外援们哭诉。但你想到她们笃定的语气，还是决定放弃传达失败的讯息。
t_en: It hurts. It hurts so much that you want to go cry at your cute aides. But when you think of their assured tone, you decide to abandon any intent of conveying your failure.

t_ch: 再想想吧。
t_en: Let's think about it.

t_ch: 白厄的看法是？
t_en: What does Phainon think?

t_ch: 你想到了在翁法罗斯认识的第一个值得信赖的伙伴，白厄，他一定会回应你的请求。
t_en: You recall the first trustworthy companion you befriended in Amphoreus, Phainon. He will surely respond to your request.

t_ch: …还有这种奇特的生物吗？我见过不少奇兽，但从未见过没有腿脚、没有肚皮的种类。
t_en: ...There's such a bizarre creature? I've seen plenty of weird beasts, but none without legs or belly.

t_ch: 居然是白厄大英雄！快回答，快回答吧！
t_en: It's the great hero Phainon! Quick, what is your answer?

t_ch: 答案或许是——某种特殊的奇美拉？
t_en: Could it be... a particular kind of chimera?

t_ch: 唔！
t_en: Ugh!

t_ch: 太好了，咬到白厄大英雄了！这辈子不刷牙了！
t_en: Fantastic! I bit the great hero Phainon! I won't brush my teeth for the rest of my life!

t_ch: 呃…虽然答错了，但它看起来很开心的样子。
t_en: Err... You got it wrong, but it looks pretty happy.

t_ch: 白厄继续思索着正确的答案，低着头离开了……
t_en: Phainon leaves with his head lowered, his mind still foraging for the right answer...

t_ch: 还是相信自己吧！
t_en: It's better to believe in yourself!

t_ch: 唉唉，本狮只好公布，正确答案是——「流言蜚语」。
t_en: Oh dear, I have no choice but to announce the right answer — it's rumors and gossip.

t_ch: 还需要解释吗？仔细想想，流言没有腿脚，却传得比什么都快吼！
t_en: You still need an explanation? Just think about it: Rumors have no legs, but can travel faster than anything else!

t_ch: 凡是有耳朵的，都爱听别人的流言；凡是有脑子的，都惧怕流言落到自己头上吼！
t_en: Those with ears love listening to the gossip of others. Those with brains fear that the rumors will befall right on top of them!

t_ch: 就这、就这？
t_en: What? This is it?

t_ch: 这情节我好像见过…
t_en: I think I've seen this scene somewhere...

t_ch: 你仿佛看到狮子得意地摇着不存在的尾巴，向你露出嘲讽的坏笑…早该想到的！答案就差写在这头爱好八卦的狮子脸上了。
t_en: It's as if you can see the lion swishing its non-existent tail, flashing a toothy, mocking grin at you... You should've thought of it! The answer was practically written all over this loose-tongued lion's face.

t_ch: 但现在，还是趁它不注意，赶紧远远地逃开吧。
t_en: But now, while it's distracted, hurry up and flee far.

t_ch: 墙上有一尊石头雕成的狮子脑袋，大张着嘴巴。
t_en: There is a sculpture of a lion's head on the wall, its maw wide open.

t_ch: 在你途经的某些世界里，狮子牙齿会被摸得发亮，路过的人很难忍住把手放进去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: In some of the worlds you traversed, the lion's teeth are usually rubbed smooth by touching hands. Passersby can't help but feel an urge to thrust their hands in its mouth. You decide to...

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 噢！真遗憾，你一定熟读文物保护法规，或者受过吉奥里亚的赐福，竟然能无视这只石狮子的存在。但仔细想想，过分谨慎是不是会错过什么？
t_en: Oh! How unfortunate. You must be versed in cultural artifact preservation rules, or once blessed by Georios — to think that you would ignore this stone lion's presence. But if you think about it, isn't being overly careful causing you to miss out on something?

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的题目考考你，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how your grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 可以不回答吗？
t_en: Can I refuse to answer?

t_ch: 哦吼？爱好智慧的人不一定拥有智慧，但不爱智慧的人一定离智慧很远吼！
t_en: O-ho? Even those in pursuit of wisdom may find it out of their reach. The ones who abhor wisdom might never even have a glimpse of it.

t_ch: 求知若渴的本狮可不会轻易放走送到嘴边的「猎物」，你已经别无选择了吼！
t_en: My thirst for knowledge will not easily let go of prey that presents itself on a silver platter! You have no other choice!

t_ch: 来吧！让你考考我。
t_en: Alright! Let's do this.

t_ch: 吼吼吼，听好了，题目里的每个字都很重要吼——
t_en: Ho-ho-ho, listen well. Every word in this question is very important...

t_ch: 某月的月初，你去购买一只标价<unbreak>20</unbreak>万利衡币的大地兽，却忘了带钱，你找大地兽商人借了<unbreak>20</unbreak>万利衡币，买走了大地兽，并约定好月末当天来还钱。
t_en: At the beginning of a certain month, you buy a dromas priced at <unbreak>200,000</unbreak> Balance Coins, but you forget your wallet. You borrow <unbreak>200,000</unbreak> Balance Coins from the dromas merchant and purchase one, promising to pay him back at the end of the month.

t_ch: 结果，大地兽的标价涨到了<unbreak>25</unbreak>万利衡币。请问，这时你应该还给商人多少钱？
t_en: But by the end of the month, the dromases' price has gone up to <unbreak>250,000</unbreak> Balance Coins. Question: How much do you owe the merchant now?

t_ch: 试着自己想想。
t_en: Try thinking about it yourself.

t_ch: 直觉告诉你，这个问题不止是题目里的数字那么简单，你决定——
t_en: Your gut tells you that this question isn't as simple as a math problem. You decide to...

t_ch: 充分利用你的金融知识。
t_en: Fully utilize your financial know-how.

t_ch: 要充分考虑利衡币和信用点之间的汇率涨跌，一个月内货币的流通速度和贬值率，大地兽商人利用资金差可能获得的投资收益，还有……
t_en: You must fully consider the fluctuation of exchange rates between Balance Coins and credits, the circulation and depreciation rates of currencies within the month, the potential yields when the dromas merchant invests his assets, and...

t_ch: 不，别把题目想复杂了，这不是「星际和平公司」向你追讨债务，省省吧！
t_en: No, don't overcomplicate the problem. This isn't the Interastral Peace Corporation hounding you to collect their debt. Stop overthinking it!

t_ch: 检查题目里是否有错别字。
t_en: Check for phonetic traps in the question.

t_ch: 你再三检查，确信自己买到的是「大地兽」，而非「太地兽」或者「大也兽」，避开了无良商家常用的骗术。
t_en: You check and double-check, confirming that what you bought is indeed a dromas, not a tromas, or a bromas, successfully avoiding any typical merchant scam tactics.

t_ch: 看来这头狮子没有在题目中设置文字陷阱，至少你还没有发现。
t_en: Looks like this lion didn't set up any wordplay traps in the question, at least to your knowledge.

t_ch: 大声地说出答案！
t_en: Speak your answer loud and proud!

t_ch: 是的，有什么好怀疑的吗，这种级别的数学题，你已经快速心算好了。
t_en: That's right, what is there to suspect? You can practically do the mental calculation for such a juvenile math problem.

t_ch: <unbreak>25</unbreak>万利衡币！
t_en: <unbreak>250,000</unbreak> Balance Coins!

t_ch: 你是比最遵纪守法的奥赫玛好公民还要「守序善良」的存在，就连{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}都不得不为你的契约精神鼓掌。
t_en: You are an existence even more Lawful Good than the most law-abiding citizens of Okhema. Even {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} would applaud your devotion to upholding an agreement.

t_ch: 错了错了，吼吼，本狮说过，题目里的「每个字」都很重要吼！
t_en: Wrong! Ho-ho, I said that EVERY WORD is essential!

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子怎么还会咬人的…可是，到底哪里出了问题啊？
t_en: Owwch! Didn't expect it to bite... But, what exactly did you get wrong?

t_ch: <unbreak>45</unbreak>万利衡币！
t_en: <unbreak>450,000</unbreak> Balance Coins!

t_ch: 真的吗？你真是这么认为的吗？在你实际遇到这种情况，并把钱掏出来之前，希望能有人来拦住你。
t_en: Really? Are you absolutely sure? If you really run into such a circumstance, I hope someone stops you before you whip out your wallet.

t_ch: 错了错了，吼吼，本狮说过，题目里的「每个字」都很重要吼！
t_en: Wrong! Ho-ho, I said that EVERY WORD is essential!

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子怎么还会咬人的…可是，到底哪里出了问题啊？
t_en: Owwch! Didn't expect it to bite... But, what exactly did you get wrong?

t_ch: <unbreak>20</unbreak>万利衡币！
t_en: <unbreak>200,000</unbreak> Balance Coins!

t_ch: 没错，你就是精打细算的数学小天才，欠债还钱，多一分都不行。
t_en: That's right, you are genius at calculating. For matters like debt settlement, precision is right up your street.

t_ch: 错了错了，吼吼，本狮说过，题目里的「每个字」都很重要吼！
t_en: Wrong! Ho-ho, I said that EVERY WORD is essential!

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子怎么还会咬人的…可是，到底哪里出了问题啊？
t_en: Owwch! Didn't expect it to bite... But, what exactly did you get wrong?

t_ch: …还是问问其他人的想法吧。
t_en: ...Better go get a second opinion.

t_ch: 使用传信石版求助。
t_en: Ask for help with the teleslate.

t_ch: 即使在折纸大学，你也从来没有系统接受过高等数学的试炼。那么，奥赫玛里的谁看起来像是数学比较好的人呢？
t_en: You never once systematically experienced the trials of advanced mathematics, even while at Paperfold University. Then, who in Okhema looks like someone who's good with numbers?

t_ch: …问问万敌？
t_en: ...Ask Mydei?

t_ch: 万敌的数学真的比你好吗？直到消息发送成功的时候，你仍有些怀疑。
t_en: Is Mydei's math really better than yours? You still feel apprehensive even as you send the message.

t_ch: 这是什么又臭又长的题目，这群狮子越来越花里胡哨了。
t_en: Such a disgustingly long-winded problem. This rabble of lions is getting creative.

t_ch: 你带走了大地兽，还找大地兽商人借了<unbreak>20</unbreak>万，加起来当然是<unbreak>40</unbreak>万利衡币…什么？那商人还敢要涨价的5万？
t_en: You took away the dromas, and borrowed <unbreak>200,000</unbreak> from the dromas merchant. Add them all up, it's <unbreak>400,000</unbreak> Balance Coins obviously... What? That weasely merchant hiked the price another <unbreak>50,000</unbreak>?

t_ch: 合情合理，只还<unbreak>40</unbreak>万！
t_en: It's very reasonable to only repay <unbreak>400,000</unbreak>!

t_ch: 错了错了，吼吼，异乡的勇士，不过如此。不接受惩罚，哪能长点见识？
t_en: Dead wrong! Ho-ho, the ability of a brave outsider exceeds {F#her}{M#his} repute. How would one learn without suffering the consequences?

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子怎么还会咬人的…可是，到底哪里出了问题啊？
t_en: Owwch! Didn't expect it to bite... But, what exactly did you get wrong?

t_ch: …抱歉，就将这惩罚当是对肉体的试炼吧。
t_en: ...Sorry, just consider this punishment a trial of the flesh.

t_ch: 万敌头也不回地离开了，你看到他在走远后挠了挠头……
t_en: Mydei leaves without looking back, and you see him scratching his head as he walks away...

t_ch: 仿佛有哪里不对。
t_en: Something's not right here.

t_ch: 不…这个计算过程，让你很想喊出「异议！」，你需要再想想。
t_en: No... This whole calculation process just makes you want to scream out "Objection!". You need to rally your thoughts.

t_ch: …问问白厄？
t_en: ...Ask Phainon?

t_ch: 不知为何，你对白厄的数学水平有着莫名的自信，或许因为他看起来像是那种会认真听课的类型？
t_en: For some reason, you are inexplicably confident in Phainon's math ability. Or maybe it's because he looks like someone who listens intently in class?

t_ch: 不小心的话，一定会认为是<unbreak>40</unbreak>万吧！
t_en: If you aren't careful, you'd think the sum is <unbreak>400,000</unbreak>!

t_ch: 但这里有个很明显的陷阱。标价的<unbreak>20</unbreak>万已经在买走大地兽时给商人了，接下来只要还上欠商人的钱，<unbreak>20</unbreak>万利衡币就好了。
t_en: But there's an obvious trap here. The price tag of <unbreak>200,000</unbreak> was already given to the merchant when you bought the dromas. You just need to pay what you owe to the merchant now — <unbreak>200,000</unbreak> Balance Coins is enough.

t_ch: 至于涨价的部分…可没有哪条法律规定这也在要还的范围内啊。
t_en: As for the price hike... nowhere in the legal constitution does it state it needs to be repaid.

t_ch: 完全明白了，就是<unbreak>20</unbreak>万！
t_en: I totally got it! It's <unbreak>200</unbreak>k!

t_ch: 错了错了，吼吼，异乡的勇士，不过如此。不接受惩罚，哪能长点见识？
t_en: Dead wrong! Ho-ho, the ability of a brave outsider exceeds {F#her}{M#his} repute. How would one learn without suffering the consequences?

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子怎么还会咬人的…可是，到底哪里出了问题啊？
t_en: Owwch! Didn't expect it to bite... But, what exactly did you get wrong?

t_ch: 抱歉！你没事吧，需要治疗吗？！
t_en: I'm sorry! Are you all right? Do you need treatment!?

t_ch: 白厄为你包扎了根本没伤的手臂，带着歉意离开了……
t_en: Phainon bandages your completely unwounded arm for you, then leaves apologetically...

t_ch: 仿佛有哪里不对。
t_en: Something's not right here.

t_ch: 太过*正确*的答案。但你很清楚，这头狮子一定不会这么轻易放过你。
t_en: An overly correct answer. But you are clear that this lion won't let you off so easily.

t_ch: …问问阿格莱雅？
t_en: ...Ask Aglaea?

t_ch: 你在通讯里向阿格莱雅复述了题目…才听完第一句，阿格莱雅便轻笑了声。
t_en: You repeat the problem to Aglaea over the comms... She chuckles right after hearing the first sentence.

t_ch: 罚款<unbreak>50</unbreak>万。
t_en: It's a <unbreak>500,000</unbreak> fine.

t_ch: 大地兽是{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}吉奥里亚{RUBY_E#}的神圣造物，只可租赁，禁止私人售卖。
t_en: Dromases are the divine creations of {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Georios{RUBY_E#}. They are only available for rental — private sale is forbidden.

t_ch: 好哇！敢私下买卖大地兽？
t_en: The gall! How dare you illegally sell dromases!

t_ch: 哦、哦吼，这、这只是故意设置的圈套…绝、绝没有亵渎泰坦神明的意思，吼吼……
t_en: Oh, oh-ho! Th—This was an intentional trap... I—I would never dare blaspheme the divine Titans, ho-ho...

t_ch: 恰当的缄默是美德，话不多说，本狮直接公布正确答案吧！
t_en: An appropriate silence is a virtue. Without further ado, I shall announce the right answer!

t_ch: 仿佛有哪里不对。
t_en: Something's not right here.

t_ch: 等等，这不是答题吗？最好还是不要把现实规则代入题目吧。
t_en: Hang on, isn't this a math question? It's best not to involve real-world rules in the problem.

t_ch: 还是相信自己吧！
t_en: It's better to believe in yourself!

t_ch: 吼吼吼！正确答案是——「根本不用还钱」！
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! The correct answer is... it doesn't cost a single coin!

t_ch: 因为交易的时间，不巧正处在「机缘月」。在奥赫玛的历法中，第十二个月份——「机缘月」，是属于{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}的月份。
t_en: That is because the date for this deal was unfortunately during the "Month of Fortune" — In Okhema's calendar, the twelfth month is the Month of Fortune, which belongs to {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 不到月份的末尾，没有人知道「机缘月」究竟有多少天，会不会突然多出一天——也就是俗称的{RUBY_B#闰日}「幽灵日」{RUBY_E#}吼！
t_en: No one knows how many days the Month of Fortune would have without reaching the end of the month. There could suddenly be an extra day... which is called a {RUBY_B#Leap Day}Spirit Day{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 而你和大地兽商人约定还钱的日期，是「月末」当天。在「幽灵日」出现前，就算是奥赫玛最睿智的法官，也没法判定契约上的「月末」究竟指的是哪一天。
t_en: And the day you and the dromas merchant arranged for repayment is on the last day of the month. Before the Spirit Day appears, even the most sagacious judge in Okhema would be unable to determine which day is the "last day of the month" the contract is referring to.

t_ch: 人不可能在两天履行同一份契约。所以，你根本不用还钱。
t_en: No one would be able to fulfill the same contract on two different days. Thus, you don't need to fork out a single coin.

t_ch: 这不是诡辩吗？
t_en: Isn't this just sophistry?

t_ch: 吼吼吼！一点小小的手段而已。本狮提醒过，题目里的每个字都很重要吼！
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Just a little trick up my proverbial sleeve. I have reminded you before that every word in the question is equally important!

t_ch: 你这根本不是数学题…
t_en: This isn't a math problem at all...
